openmeter/app/custominvoicing.purpose: Webhook-driven marketplace billing app: the App type (this folder) implements billing.InvoicingApp + billing.InvoicingAppAsyncSyncer, deferring meaningful invoice logic to external systems via the httpdriver/ HTTP callbacks and appcustominvoicing.Service (service/), with persistence in adapter/. Primary constraint: invoice state-machine progression is gated on external metadata stamps rather than DB flags.

patterns.name: Composite Service interface
patterns.description: Service = CustomerDataService + FactoryService + SyncService. New capability areas add a sub-interface and embed it — never add methods directly to Service.
patterns.example: type Service interface { CustomerDataService; FactoryService; SyncService }

patterns.name: Advance-gate via metadata keys
patterns.description: CanDraftSyncAdvance/CanIssuingSyncAdvance return (true, nil) when MetadataKeyDraftSyncedAt/MetadataKeyFinalizedAt are present in invoice.Metadata, or when the hook is disabled via Configuration bool. New sync hooks follow this exact two-check pattern.
patterns.example:
if !a.Configuration.EnableDraftSyncHook { return true, nil }
if _, ok := invoice.Metadata[MetadataKeyDraftSyncedAt]; ok { return true, nil }

patterns.name: Validate() on every input struct
patterns.description: Input types implement Validate() returning models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)); Service/Sync methods call input.Validate() as the first statement.

patterns.name: Factory self-registers with the app marketplace
patterns.description: NewFactory calls config.AppService.RegisterMarketplaceListing(ctx, app.RegistryItem{Listing: MarketplaceListing, Factory: fact}) during construction; omitting this makes the app type invisible to the marketplace.

patterns.name: App delegates to Service, never to Adapter directly
patterns.description: App receiver methods (GetCustomerData, UpsertCustomerData, DeleteCustomerData, UpdateAppConfig) call a.customInvoicingService.*; App never imports the adapter package.
patterns.example: func (a App) GetCustomerData(ctx, input) (app.CustomerData, error) { return a.customInvoicingService.GetCustomerData(ctx, ...) }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertions at package level
patterns.description: var _ customerapp.App = (*App)(nil); var _ billing.InvoicingApp = (*App)(nil); var _ billing.InvoicingAppAsyncSyncer = (*App)(nil) — one assertion per satisfied interface in app.go.

patterns.name: InvoicingApp methods are mostly no-ops
patterns.description: ValidateStandardInvoice, UpsertStandardInvoice, DeleteStandardInvoice are intentional no-ops; only FinalizeStandardInvoice generates a non-draft number after CanIssuingSyncAdvance passes AND the draft prefix still matches.
patterns.example: func (a App) UpsertStandardInvoice(ctx, invoice) (*billing.UpsertStandardInvoiceResult, error) { return nil, nil }

key_file_guides.role: App struct, Meta (AppBase + Configuration), compile-time assertions, and all InvoicingApp + InvoicingAppAsyncSyncer methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FinalizeStandardInvoice must keep both guards: CanIssuingSyncAdvance check AND DraftInvoiceSequenceNumber.PrefixMatches before GenerateInvoiceSequenceNumber.

key_file_guides.role: Declares Service = CustomerDataService + FactoryService + SyncService — source of truth for exposed business operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add methods directly here — embed a new sub-interface instead.

key_file_guides.role: SyncService input types (SyncDraftInvoiceInput, SyncIssuingInvoiceInput, HandlePaymentTriggerInput) with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: UpsertInvoiceResults and FinalizeInvoiceResult are required pointer fields — validate non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: Factory + NewFactory (registers marketplace listing), InstallApp, UninstallApp, NewApp; MarketplaceListing defines capabilities.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NewFactory must call RegisterMarketplaceListing; InstallApp must call NewApp after CreateApp to return a fully wired App.

key_file_guides.role: CustomerData type (implements app.CustomerData) and App bridge methods to appcustominvoicing.Service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add billing.Service calls here — customer data operations must stay service-only.

key_file_guides.role: Adapter = CustomerDataAdapter + AppConfigAdapter + TxCreator. Implemented by adapter/ with soft-delete and upserts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetAppConfiguration/GetCustomerData return zero-value on NotFound — callers expect that, not an error.

anti_patterns: Calling billing.Service directly from App methods — all operations must go through appcustominvoicing.Service.
Returning an error for NotFound from GetAppConfiguration — the adapter returns zero-value Configuration; callers expect that.
Skipping input.Validate() at the start of any SyncService method.
Adding new sync-hook logic without a corresponding CanXxxAdvance metadata-key gate and Configuration bool.
Giving the no-op InvoicingApp methods (ValidateStandardInvoice, UpsertStandardInvoice, DeleteStandardInvoice) real side-effects — they must remain no-ops.

decisions.decision: InvoicingApp methods are mostly no-ops; meaningful logic flows through SyncService HTTP endpoints.
decisions.rationale: External systems drive invoice lifecycle via webhook callbacks to httpdriver endpoints, not via state-machine hooks; only FinalizeStandardInvoice generates the non-draft number as a final step.

decisions.decision: Advance gates check invoice metadata rather than a DB flag.
decisions.rationale: Metadata is already on the in-memory invoice passed to FinalizeStandardInvoice, avoiding an extra DB round-trip and keeping the gate stateless.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new sync hook that blocks advance until an external system stamps metadata

openmeter/app/stripe.purpose: Stripe marketplace billing app: the App type (this folder, split across appinvoice.go/appcustomer.go/clientapp.go/config.go) implements billing.InvoicingApp and customerapp.App, backed by appstripe.Service (service/), persistence (adapter/), and the Stripe API client factories (client/), with all Stripe HTTP endpoints in httpdriver/. Primary constraint: Stripe is the source of truth for invoice numbers and all Stripe errors flow through providerError translation.

patterns.name: Four-file App method split
patterns.description: App methods split by concern: appinvoice.go (InvoicingApp), appcustomer.go (customerapp.App + CustomerData), clientapp.go (getStripeClient helper), config.go (UpdateAppConfig). New App methods go in the matching file.

patterns.name: getStripeClient encapsulates secret retrieval
patterns.description: App methods needing a Stripe client call a.getStripeClient(ctx, operationName, logKV...) which fetches AppData + secret + builds StripeAppClient. Never fetch the API key secret inline.

patterns.name: UpsertStandardInvoice branches on ExternalIDs.Invoicing
patterns.description: Empty ExternalIDs.Invoicing -> createInvoice; otherwise -> updateInvoice. The result always sets ExternalID + InvoiceNumber from Stripe's response (Stripe is source of truth for numbers).
patterns.example:
if invoice.ExternalIDs.Invoicing == "" { return a.createInvoice(ctx, invoice) }
return a.updateInvoice(ctx, invoice)

patterns.name: StripeCalculator for all currency arithmetic
patterns.description: Every amount sent to Stripe goes through NewStripeCalculator(invoice.Currency).RoundToAmount(). Never pass raw float64 or alpacadecimal to Stripe params.

patterns.name: HandleInvoiceStateTransition idempotency guards
patterns.description: Service.HandleInvoiceStateTransition requires both TargetStatuses (already-in-target = skip) and IgnoreInvoiceInStatus (out-of-order = skip). New Stripe webhook handlers must supply both guards.

patterns.name: Input namespace cross-check
patterns.description: Input types carrying both AppID and CustomerID validate AppID.Namespace == CustomerID.Namespace. Apply the same check to any new input combining two namespaced IDs.
patterns.example: if i.AppID.Namespace != i.CustomerID.Namespace { return errors.New("app and customer must be in the same namespace") }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertions per file
patterns.description: Each file implementing an interface declares var _ <Interface> = (*App)(nil) at the top; add an assertion whenever App gains a new interface.

key_file_guides.role: Full InvoicingApp impl: Validate/Upsert/Finalize/Delete StandardInvoice plus createInvoice/updateInvoice helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: updateInvoice builds stripeLinesToRemove from existing line items — new line attributes must update both add and update paths; AddInvoiceLines resolves line IDs via a separate ListInvoiceLineItems call.
ValidateCustomerByID switches on CollectionMethod (ChargeAutomatically vs SendInvoice); new collection methods need a new case.

key_file_guides.role: StripeCalculator: alpacadecimal -> Stripe integer subunits (RoundToAmount) + display formatting.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RoundToAmount multiplies by 10^subunits and truncates to int64 — only for amounts going to Stripe, not intermediate billing math.

key_file_guides.role: Input/output structs with Validate(), API/Webhook secret key constants, HandleInvoiceStateTransitionInput (Target/Ignore status + hooks).
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateCheckoutSessionInput has mutually exclusive fields (CreateCustomerInput vs CustomerID) validated inline — new options need similar mutual-exclusion checks.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface = AppFactoryService + StripeAppService + CustomerService + BillingService sub-interfaces.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New Stripe operations go in the matching sub-interface — never add methods directly to Service.

key_file_guides.role: StripeMarketplaceListing var and the three capability vars.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Capability keys are stored in DB; renaming them is a breaking migration.

key_file_guides.role: AppCheckoutSessionEvent implements watermill marshaler.Event with versioned metadata.EventType (subsystem 'app.stripe').
key_file_guides.watch_for: New domain events must follow the same EventName()/EventMetadata() pattern and be versioned (v1, v2, ...).

anti_patterns: Calling Stripe API methods without routing errors through the client's providerError() — misses the 401 app-status update and domain error mapping.
Creating Stripe objects without injecting StripeMetadataNamespace/StripeMetadataAppID in Metadata — breaks reconciliation.
Using raw float64 or alpacadecimal for Stripe amounts instead of StripeCalculator.RoundToAmount.
Adding HandleInvoiceStateTransition webhook handlers without TargetStatuses and IgnoreInvoiceInStatus guards — causes duplicate state transitions.
Implementing App business logic in the adapter sub-package — adapter is pure persistence; orchestration belongs in service/.

decisions.decision: Two separate Stripe client interfaces: StripeClient (pre-install OAuth/webhook setup) vs StripeAppClient (post-install billing).
decisions.rationale: Pre-install calls need only OAuth scopes; post-install needs API key + app context. Splitting prevents using an unauthenticated client for billing operations.

decisions.decision: Service self-registers into the marketplace in New() rather than at wire time.
decisions.rationale: Keeps registration co-located with factory construction; Wire only calls New() and needs no knowledge of the marketplace registry.

decisions.decision: Stripe is the source of truth for invoice numbers — FinalizeStandardInvoice reads stripeInvoice.Number and stores it.
decisions.rationale: Stripe's numbering is deterministic and customer-facing; generating a separate number would cause reconciliation mismatch.

code_examples.scenario: Add a Stripe webhook handler that transitions an invoice with idempotency guards
